headline: Bato We'll seek 'judicial relief' over ICC drug war probe  GMA News Online
text:
After the Justice Department expressed openness to engage with the International Criminal Court (ICC) over its investigation into former President Rodrigo Duterte's drug war, Senator Ronald "Bato" dela Rosa said on Monday that they would seek "judicial relief" from the Supreme Court if the government cooperated with the probe.
"Go ahead kung anong gusto nila. Hindi namin sila maco-control… Basta ako, very solid ‘yung aking paniniwala na walang jurisdiction ang ICC sa ating bansa," Dela Rosa said.
(They can go ahead with whatever they want. We cannot control them. As for me, my firm belief is that the ICC has no jurisdiction in our country.) 
"Pumasok kayo dito, ICC. We will go to the Supreme Court, we will seek judicial relief kung talaga bang may jurisdiction ang ICC dito," he added.  
(Come in here, ICC. We will go to the Supreme Court, and we will seek judicial relief to determine if the ICC truly has jurisdiction here.)
As the former Philippine National Police (PNP) chief during the Duterte administration's drug war, Dela Rosa reiterated that he would not acknowledge any communications from the ICC, as doing so would imply recognition of its authority.
Dela Rosa also said that the ICC's insistence that it investigate the drug war despite the Philippines' withdrawal from the Rome Statute—the treaty that established the ICC—showed that the investigation was less about justice and more about the ICC's "control" of member states.
Meanwhile, Dela Rosa defended the drug war, calling it a "great success" and citing stories from Filipinos who thanked him for making the country safer.
Government records indicate that around 6,200 individuals were killed during anti-drug operations. However, human rights organizations estimate the death toll could be as high as 30,000 due to unreported but related killings.
The Philippines formally withdrew from the Rome Statute in 2019, after the tribunal initiated a probe into the drug war.
In January 2023, the ICC authorized the reopening of its inquiry, which had been suspended in November 2021. 
By July 2023, the ICC Appeals Chamber denied the Philippine government's plea to halt the investigation, prompting many officials to criticize continued engagement with the tribunal.
President Ferdinand "Bongbong" Marcos Jr. affirmed that the government would not enforce any ICC arrest warrant against Duterte. 
He also reiterated his stance that the ICC lacks jurisdiction in the Philippines and poses a threat to the nation's sovereignty. — DVM, GMA Integrated News

headline: Chito Victolero lauds young guns as Magnolia fights for playoffs  GMA News Online
text:
Magnolia head coach Chito Victolero couldn't be happier that his team has finally fielded a healthy roster as they look to make a final push to reach the playoffs.
But the longtime tactician said he was more than satisfied to see everyone, especially the young guns, to step up amid their difficult situation in the PBA Season 49 Commissioner's Cup.
"[We are] having the confidence to go to war with a complete lineup now. I think this is the first time that we’re complete, we’re all healthy because in the first five games, our import was injured and then after that Paul Lee was in and out of the lineup then most of the players may mga nararamdaman," Victolero said.
"But now I’m very confident since this is the first time na complete kami with a healthy lineup."
Victolero liked what he saw when the likes of rookies Jerom Lastimosa and Peter Alfaro as well as sophomore Zav Lucero stepped up big time against higher-ranked Eastern during their clash on Sunday.
With Lee still not reaching his top form and Barroca sitting out most of the second half after hurting his eye, the rookie backcourt tandem of Lastimosa and Alfaro provided quality minutes while Lucero proved to be a threat in offense.
Both Lastimosa and Lucero finished with 20 points each while Alfaro chipped in four markers and two rebounds in a starter role.
"We need the young guns, not just the veterans, because ayun nga, it’s a collective effort from everyone," Victolero added.
"I told them it’s not one or two or three players who need to step up for this game but everyone, including our utility guys. We need everyone from the team."
Magnolia will need more than this when they take on Meralco this Friday in another do-or-die game. And they expect nothing but a war.
"Sabi ko sa kanila, let’s give it a try, let’s give our best effort. We need  to prepare well for our last game, which is Meralco. We know na it’s a war."
—JMB, GMA Integrated News

headline: Gloria Romero's grandson, daughter on late veteran actress 'Until the last moment, she was still trying to give'  GMA News Online
text:
Chris and Maritess Gutierrez, the grandson and daughter of Gloria Romero, paid tribute to the late veteran actress and thanked all of her supporters amid her passing.
On Monday, "Fast Talk with Boy Abunda" aired statements from Chris and Maritess taken during the wake of the actress, where Chris recalled one of the last conversations he had with his grandmother.
"She said, 'Chris matanda na 'ko, wala na kong mabibigay sa 'yo, nabigay ko na sa 'yo lahat' and then I was telling her, 'Lola andami mo na nabigay sa amin, it's okay, you already gave us so much. Thank you for everything,'" he said.
"Until the last moment, she was still trying to give pa rin. That's the kind of person my lola is, you can see it naman po, that's why everybody loves her," he added.
Maritess, for her part, shared that it was all in God's plan for her to be Gloria's daughter.
"I believe that God was the one who gave my mom to me, to take care of me," she said. "It was our destiny and also for Chris. It's all God's plan."
She also thanked all of her mother's supporters, who comprise a diverse mix of the older and younger generation.
"Thank you everybody for loving my mom. I mean, for 70 years she's been in the industry, nung araw 'yung mga senior people, ngayon mga bata because of GMA nabigyan siya ng ['Daig Kayo ng Lola Ko']. So, ang lawak ng age gaps ng mga fans ni mama from matanda to children."
Echoing Chris, Maritess added that her mom was able to give so much of herself up to her final years.
"I think my mom was able to deliver and give herself until the last, up to her 70th year. Imagine hanggang bata naabutan pa nila si mama. Me and Chris, we thank everybody for that. Salamat," she said.
Gloria passed away on January 25 at 91 years old, as announced by Maritess via Facebook.
Her wake is being held at Arlington Memorial Chapel in Quezon City, with a public viewing scheduled on January 27 and 28, from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m.
Hailed as the "Queen of Philippine Cinema," Gloria has won various acting awards throughout her 70-year career in showbiz. Among her memorable performance in film are "Magnifico," "Dalagang Ilocana," "Tarima," "Tanging Yaman," and "Rainbow's Sunset."
She was given a star-studded tribute in February 2024, and in April of the same year was named by the Film Development Council of the Philippines' among its Lifetime Achievement Honorees.
In the wake of her passing, celebrities and personalities like Barbie Forteza, Vilma Santos, director Mark Reyes, Charo Santos, and more paid tribute to the veteran actress.
— CDC, GMA Integrated News

headline: PNP tells Comelec 'Oplan Katok' not intended to harass anyone  GMA News Online
text:
The Philippine National Police told the Commission on Elections (COMELEC) Monday that the “Oplan Katok,” a door-to-door campaign against expired firearms, is not intended to harass or intimidate anyone amid the election season.
PNP spokesperson Police Brigadier General Jean Fajardo maintained that the said operation is a “regular function” of the police body.
“Regular function po ito as part of regulatory power, authority ng PNP. Particularly kapag mayroon tayong expired licenses po, bahagi ng ginagawa nating intervention ay puntahan itong mga nag-expire na pong licenses,” said Fajardo at a press briefing.
(It is a regular function as part of the regulatory power and authority of PNP. Our intervention to those with expired gun licenses is to visit them door-to-door.) 
“We want to assure the Comelec ay hindi po ito sinimulan dahil sa election po. Even prior to the start of election ay ginagawa na po natin.”
(We want to assure the Comelec that this has started even prior to the start of the election season.)
The PNP made the statement after Comelec chairperson George Garcia said in reports that such operations could lead to abuse of powers by the policemen.
But the police official said that it is willing to sit down with the Comelec to discuss “Oplan Katok.”
“We will be coordinating and sitting down and discussing with Comelec to inform them of the nature itong ginagawa nating Oplan Katok and we want to assure the Comelec, this is not intended to harass anyone po,” said Fajardo.
(We will be coordinating, sitting down, and discussing with Comelec to inform them of the nature of the Oplan Katok, and we want to assure the Comelec that this is not intended to harass anyone po.)
“We want to assure everyone, particularly the Comelec, this is not intended to harass or coerce or intimidate anyone,” she added.
Data by PNP showed that 621,395 notices were issued to gun owners in 2024. Of this number, 16,473 have renewed their gun licenses and 10,676 firearms were turned over for safe keeping. — RSJ, GMA Integrated News

headline: Rondae Hollis-Jefferson shines with triple-double on idol Kobe Bryant’s death anniversary  GMA News Online
text:
Rondae Hollis-Jefferson picked a good day to honor his idol.
The resident TNT import detonated for a rare triple-double against no less than defending champion San Miguel on Sunday, which also marked the five-year anniversary of the passing of his hero, Kobe Bryant.
Hollis-Jefferson delivered a masterful outing with his 35 points, 21 rebounds, and 10 assists as the Tropang Giga torched the Beermen, 115-97, on the way to an 8-3 card. They also sent San Miguel to the brink of elimination with a 5-7 slate.
"We all know how I feel about Kobe and how much I admire his game," Hollis-Jefferson said.
"Like I said during the World Cup, he’s definitely with me. I watched him, studied his game, and he was just a workhorse."
Hollis-Jefferson shared he has been trying to embody Bryant's love of the game as well as his work ethic, which led the latter to five championships and a number of individual accolades during his colorful 20-year stint with the Los Angeles Lakers in the NBA.
And with Bryant in his heart and mind, Hollis-Jefferson hopes to lead TNT to another crown.
"At the end of the day, he really cared about the game, he cared about winning and that’s what I try to do every time I get on the court—trying to help my teammates be successful whether before you even come to the game or practice, things like that," Hollis-Jefferson added.
"Just trying to be a leader and I think that’s why we’re successful."
—JMB, GMA Integrated News

headline: Cloudy skies, rains expected over parts of PH  GMA News Online
text:
The shear line will bring rains over Palawan and Visayas while the Northeast Monsoon (Amihan) will affect Luzon in the next 24 hours, PAGASA said Monday afternoon.
In its weather forecast, the state weather bureau said Visayas, Palawan, Dinagat Islands, and Surigao del Norte will see cloudy skies with scattered rains and isolated thunderstorms due to the shear line.
Cloudy skies with scattered rains and thunderstorms will be observed throughout the rest of Mindanao due to the easterlies.
Meanwhile, cloudy skies with rains will persist over Batanes and Cagayan due to the Amihan.
These moderate to at times heavy rains may cause flash floods or landslides.
The Amihan will also bring partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated light rains over Metro Manila and the rest of Luzon. 
State meteorologists said winds will be strong to gale while coastal waters will be rough to very rough in Northern Luzon.
Meanwhile, Central Luzon will see moderate to strong wind conditions and moderate to rough coastal water conditions.
Winds will be light to moderate while coastal waters will be slight to moderate throughout the rest of the country.
The sun will rise at 6:25 a.m. on Tuesday. — Mariel Celine Serquiña/BM, GMA Integrated News

headline: Cops plead not guilty over 2022 drug mess  GMA News Online
text:
Several cops on Monday pleaded not guilty to the charges filed against them in connection with the 990 kilograms of suspected shabu that was seized in Manila in 2022, according to Manny Vargas report on Super Radyo dzBB.
 
The cops pleaded not guilty before the Manila Regional Trial Court (RTC) Branch 175.
 
Mga pulis na akusado sa 990 kilos ng shabu na na-recover sa isang gusali sa Tondo, Maynila noong 2022, naghain ng not guilty plea sa Manila Regional Trial Court. | via @VargasMannysen pic.twitter.com/k3bIvCX3L7
 
Meanwhile, Police Master Sergeant Rodolfo Mayo Jr. reportedly did not enter any plea due to his pending motion to quash.
Due to this, the court entered a not guilty plea for Mayo.
The RTC also reportedly tackled the motion of the accused on whether or not they will be moved to the Philippine National Police Custodial Center.
This included Patrolman James Osalvo, who asked the court to allow him to be transferred to the PNP General Hospital due to a medical condition.
Meanwhile, the court set the pre-trial for the case on February 14.
A panel of prosecutors has filed charges against the cops, including two ranking police generals, for violations of the Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act of 2002.
The cops’ supposed violations include the planting of evidence and the delay and bungling in the prosecution of the drug cases.
The Philippine National Police earlier announced the 22 police officers would be placed under restrictive custody after charges were filed against them over the supposed mishandling of the P6.7 billion drug haul in October 2022.  
The cops were also disarmed. —Joahna Lei Casilao/LDF, GMA Integrated News

headline: Palace confirms executive clemency granted to Jed Mabilog  GMA News Online
text:
Malacañang on Monday confirmed that President Ferdinand ''Bongbong'' Marcos Jr. has granted executive clemency to former Iloilo City mayor Jed Mabilog. 
''Yes, that's true,'' Executive Lucas Bersamin told Palace reporters when asked for confirmation on the matter. 
In a separate statement, Bersamin said the granting of executive clemency was in view of Mabilog's long standing commitment to good governance, coupled with awards and recognition received by Iloilo City under his leadership.
Bersamin said Marcos has granted Mabilog's petition for executive clemency ''in connection with his administrative case, thereby removing the penalties or disabilities resulting from such case.''
The Presidential Communications Office said the executive clemency was in relation to the decision of the Office of the Ombudsman last October 23, 2017, dismissing Mabilog after finding him guilty of serious dishonesty for his failure to explain his alleged questionable wealth worth P8.9 million from 2012 to 2013.
Bersamin, meanwhile, answered in affirmative when asked if Mabilog could run again for government post after the executive clemency was granted. 
Meanwhile, Mabilog is facing another graft charges before the Sandiganbayan filed against him and councilor Plaridel Nava II by the Office of the Ombudsman in 2023 over their alleged intervention in the awarding of a government contract to a towing services firm where they have an interest in.
To recall, it was in September last year when Mabilog posted bail over the graft charges.
Former President Rodrigo Duterte included Mabilog in his drug watch list in 2016. Mabilog has since maintained his innocence, saying he found his inclusion in the list "very unfortunate and disconcerting."
In a separate interview, Bersamin said the accusation against Mabilog on illegal drugs is already irrelevant.
''President Duterte accused him of being the drug lord in Iloilo City, whether that is true or not, that is irrelevant now,'' Bersamin said.
'Vindication'
In a separate statement sent to reporters, Mabilog thanked Marcos for granting him executive clemency, noting that what happened was a vindication from the wrongful cases filed against him.
''I am personally grateful to God and Pres. Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. for granting me this executive clemency,'' Mabilog said. ''This serves not just as a vindication for the wrongful and deceitful cases filed against me but as the triumph of justice in this country.''
He said his seven years of ''exile'' taught him lessons such as the importance of love and integrity in building and rebuilding the community.
''This administration upholds justice, which people like me, who are unjustly accused, can somehow be confident to avail themselves of vindication,'' Mabilog said.
Meanwhile, former chief presidential legal counsel Salvador Panelo said the pardon granted on Mabilog was a ''reward for attacking and besmirching the integrity and reputation of former Rodrigo Roa Duterte, who is a critic of the administration.'' —AOL/RSJ, GMA Integrated News
